newDirectories.name: Container orchestration
newDirectories.description: Kubernetes distributions, managed clusters, and container schedulers for running workloads at scale.
newDirectories.heroCopy: Cost, DX, ecosystem, scalability, and ops burden decide whether your cluster runs itself or runs you ragged.

aspects.label: Cost
aspects.description: Control-plane fees and total cost relative to what you get (5 = great value).

aspects.label: Developer experience
aspects.description: CLI, dashboards, day-one setup, and ergonomics of running workloads.

aspects.label: Ecosystem
aspects.description: Add-ons, operators, integrations, and community depth.

aspects.label: Scalability
aspects.description: Headroom for node count, multi-cluster, and large production traffic.

aspects.label: Ops burden
aspects.description: How little upgrade, patching, and maintenance toil it demands (5 = nearly hands-off).

items.name: Amazon EKS
items.description: AWS's managed Kubernetes service with deep IAM, VPC, and AWS service integration.

items.name: Google GKE
items.description: Google's managed Kubernetes engine with Autopilot mode and strong autoscaling and upgrades.

items.name: Azure AKS
items.description: Microsoft's managed Kubernetes service with Entra ID identity and event-driven scaling.

items.name: Red Hat OpenShift
items.description: Enterprise Kubernetes distribution with built-in CI/CD, developer tooling, and hardened security defaults.

items.name: Rancher
items.description: Open-source multi-cluster Kubernetes management platform spanning on-prem and public clouds.

items.name: k3s
items.description: Rancher's lightweight CNCF-certified Kubernetes distribution packaged as a single binary for edge and IoT.

items.name: HashiCorp Nomad
items.description: Simple, flexible scheduler that orchestrates containerized and non-containerized workloads on a single binary.

items.name: Docker Swarm
items.description: Docker's built-in clustering and orchestration mode for simple multi-host container deployments.

items.name: DigitalOcean Kubernetes
items.description: Managed Kubernetes with a free control plane and DigitalOcean's developer-friendly pricing and UX.

items.name: Akamai LKE
items.description: Akamai Cloud's managed Kubernetes (formerly Linode) with a free control plane and predictable pricing.

items.name: Oracle OKE
items.description: Oracle Cloud's managed Kubernetes engine with a free basic control plane and OCI integration.

items.name: Talos Linux
items.description: Minimal immutable Linux OS managed entirely through an API for secure, reproducible Kubernetes nodes.

items.name: RKE2
items.description: Rancher's security-focused, FIPS-compliant Kubernetes distribution for government and enterprise clusters.

items.name: kind
items.description: Tool for running local Kubernetes clusters inside Docker containers, built primarily for testing and CI.

items.name: Portainer
items.description: Lightweight management UI for Docker, Swarm, and Kubernetes environments aimed at smaller teams.

newDirectories.name: Infrastructure as code
newDirectories.description: Declarative and programmatic tools for provisioning cloud infrastructure from version-controlled definitions.
newDirectories.heroCopy: Cost, DX, ecosystem, multi-cloud reach, and state management separate clean provisioning from drift and dread.

aspects.label: Cost
aspects.description: Licensing and platform cost relative to value (5 = free or cheap).

aspects.label: Developer experience
aspects.description: Authoring ergonomics, language fit, docs, and debuggability.

aspects.label: Ecosystem
aspects.description: Providers, modules, registries, and community size.

aspects.label: Multi-cloud
aspects.description: Breadth of providers and how cloud-agnostic the tool is.

aspects.label: State management
aspects.description: Quality of state, drift detection, and reconciliation handling.

items.name: Terraform
items.description: HashiCorp's HCL-based provisioning tool with the largest provider ecosystem under a BSL license.

items.name: OpenTofu
items.description: Open-source, community-governed fork of Terraform offering the same HCL, providers, and workflow.

items.name: Pulumi
items.description: Infrastructure as code using real programming languages like TypeScript, Python, and Go.

items.name: AWS CDK
items.description: AWS Cloud Development Kit for defining CloudFormation infrastructure in familiar programming languages.

items.name: Ansible
items.description: Red Hat's agentless automation tool that configures and provisions systems over SSH with YAML playbooks.

items.name: Crossplane
items.description: CNCF control plane that extends Kubernetes to provision and manage cloud infrastructure as custom resources.

items.name: SST
items.description: TypeScript framework for deploying full-stack apps to AWS with high-level constructs and live local dev.

items.name: Terragrunt
items.description: Thin wrapper around Terraform and OpenTofu that reduces repetition and manages dependencies across modules.

items.name: AWS CloudFormation
items.description: AWS's native declarative provisioning service that manages stacks of resources from JSON or YAML templates.

items.name: Azure Bicep
items.description: Microsoft's domain-specific language that compiles to ARM templates for declarative Azure provisioning.

items.name: CDK for Terraform
items.description: HashiCorp's CDKTF for defining Terraform infrastructure using TypeScript, Python, and other languages.

items.name: Terramate
items.description: Orchestration and code-generation layer that adds stacks, change detection, and automation to Terraform.

items.name: Atlantis
items.description: Open-source application that automates Terraform pull-request workflows with plan and apply via comments.

items.name: env0
items.description: Infrastructure-as-code management platform with self-service, governance, and cost controls for Terraform and friends.

items.name: Scalr
items.description: Remote state and operations backend for Terraform and OpenTofu with policy enforcement and hierarchy.

items.name: Winglang
items.description: Cloud-oriented programming language that compiles infrastructure and runtime code together for serverless apps.

newDirectories.name: API clients & testing
newDirectories.description: Request clients and automated testing tools for building, exploring, and validating HTTP and other APIs.
newDirectories.heroCopy: Cost, DX, ecosystem, protocol support, and automation decide whether your API workflow flows or fights you.

aspects.label: Cost
aspects.description: Pricing relative to value, including free and open-source tiers (5 = great value).

aspects.label: Developer experience
aspects.description: Interface speed, ergonomics, and how pleasant requests feel to build.

aspects.label: Ecosystem
aspects.description: Integrations, plugins, collaboration, and community depth.

aspects.label: Protocol support
aspects.description: Breadth across REST, GraphQL, gRPC, WebSocket, and more.

aspects.label: Automation
aspects.description: Scripting, CI integration, and automated/contract testing capability.

items.name: Postman
items.description: The dominant API platform combining a request client with collections, mocks, monitors, and collaboration.

items.name: Insomnia
items.description: Kong's fast API client for REST, GraphQL, gRPC, and WebSocket with environments and CI-friendly workflows.

items.name: Bruno
items.description: Open-source, Git-native API client that stores collections as plain text files in your repository.

items.name: Hoppscotch
items.description: Open-source, browser-native API client that loads instantly with no install or account required.

items.name: Thunder Client
items.description: Lightweight REST API client extension that runs directly inside VS Code without a separate app.

items.name: Hurl
items.description: Command-line tool that runs and asserts HTTP requests defined in a plain-text format, ideal for CI.

items.name: Schemathesis
items.description: Property-based API testing tool that generates test cases from OpenAPI and GraphQL schemas to find bugs.

items.name: Grafana k6
items.description: Developer-centric load and performance testing tool that scripts API tests in JavaScript.

items.name: HTTPie
items.description: Human-friendly command-line and desktop HTTP client designed for clear, intuitive API interaction.

items.name: Karate
items.description: Open-source framework combining API test automation, mocks, and performance testing in a readable DSL.

items.name: REST Assured
items.description: Java DSL library for testing and validating REST services inside JVM test suites.

items.name: Pact
items.description: Consumer-driven contract testing framework that verifies microservice integrations without full environments.

items.name: SoapUI
items.description: Veteran open-source tool for functional and security testing of SOAP and REST web services.

items.name: Step CI
items.description: Open-source, language-agnostic API testing and monitoring tool configured with simple declarative YAML.

items.name: Apidog
items.description: All-in-one API platform combining design, debugging, mocking, and automated testing in one client.

newDirectories.name: Internal tools
newDirectories.description: Low-code platforms for building admin panels, dashboards, and internal apps on top of your data.
newDirectories.heroCopy: Cost, DX, ecosystem, customization, and data connectors determine how fast an internal app ships and how far it bends.

aspects.label: Cost
aspects.description: Pricing and seat fees relative to value, including open-source options (5 = great value).

aspects.label: Developer experience
aspects.description: Builder ergonomics, scripting, and how quickly developers ship apps.

aspects.label: Ecosystem
aspects.description: Components, templates, integrations, and community size.

aspects.label: Customization
aspects.description: How freely you can extend UI, logic, and styling beyond the defaults.

aspects.label: Data connectors
aspects.description: Breadth of database, API, and SaaS sources it connects to out of the box.

items.name: Retool
items.description: Polished low-code platform for building internal tools with enterprise SSO, audit logs, and granular permissions.

items.name: Appsmith
items.description: Widely-adopted open-source low-code builder for dashboards and CRUD apps with drag-and-drop UI and JavaScript.

items.name: ToolJet
items.description: Developer-first open-source platform for building internal tools, workflows, and AI-powered apps.

items.name: Budibase
items.description: Open-source low-code platform focused on quickly building internal apps and admin panels with minimal logic.

items.name: Superblocks
items.description: Enterprise internal-app platform combining UI building, backend workflows, and centralized governance.

items.name: UI Bakery
items.description: Low-code internal tool builder for dashboards and workflows with a polished developer experience.

items.name: ILLA
items.description: Open-source low-code platform for building AI-driven dashboards, admin panels, and internal tools.

items.name: Forest Admin
items.description: Admin panel platform that auto-generates internal tools by connecting to your databases and APIs.

items.name: Refine
items.description: Open-source React framework for rapidly building data-heavy internal tools, dashboards, and admin panels.

items.name: React Admin
items.description: Mature open-source React framework for building B2B admin applications on top of REST and GraphQL APIs.

items.name: NocoDB
items.description: Open-source Airtable alternative that turns any database into a smart spreadsheet and internal app interface.

items.name: Baserow
items.description: Open-source no-code database platform for building internal tools and collaborative apps from your data.

items.name: Lowdefy
items.description: Open-source, config-driven framework for building internal web apps and admin panels from YAML.

items.name: Jet Admin
items.description: No-code platform for building internal tools, dashboards, and customer portals on top of existing data.
